title: eryngium fluminense
intro: Eryngium fluminense, also known as the 'Brazilian sea holly', is a flowering plant native to Brazil, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of eryngium fluminense include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing relief from allergies and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
